page: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM-based server instance belonging to the DPSv5 family. It is powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor based on the ARMv8 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The instance features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading-related resource sharing. It is equipped with 208.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.25 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, relying on external network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory bandwidth for cached operations. The instance achieved a strong rating in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Cached small-block memory bandwidth read-only tests also registered a strong result at 4385.45 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write operations fell into the average tier. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks scoring in the weak category. However, the instance demonstrated strong performance in specialized tasks such as software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, which reached 80.8 million operations per second.

The D64ps_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency by utilizing power-efficient ARM architecture to deliver high physical core density. This makes it highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated, non-shared CPU cores, such as software compilation, web serving, and Redis-based caching. It is less suited for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring high-performance local storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM-based server instance from the DPSv5 family, powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor with 64 dedicated physical cores and 208.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and Redis SET throughput, alongside high cached memory bandwidth of 4385.45 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core execution and text processing represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the lower tiers. The instance provides a cost-efficient, high-density hardware profile well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, and caching applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM-based server instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores, 208.0 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance, making it highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM-based server with 64 dedicated physical cores and 208.0 GB of RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density, ARM-powered server with dedicated physical cores and strong multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor running at 3.0 GHz
64 dedicated physical cores with single-thread execution
208.0 GB of system memory (3.25 GB per core)
Strong multi-core performance and software compilation benchmarks
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or GPUs
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, and Redis caching
